<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>The three-body problem</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Newton 2 Restricted & Lagrange 3 Special solutions 4 Poincaré tangle 5 KAM 6 Applications

The three-body problem

Three masses, gravity, and the ten classical integrals — and the surprise that what's left is enough to invent dynamical systems, KAM theory, and modern celestial mechanics.

blocks.html:
1. Newton's equations and conserved quantities

Three point masses $m_1,m_2,m_3$ at positions $r_1,r_2,r_3\in\mathbb{R}^3$, interacting only by gravity, satisfy $$\ddot r_i \;=\; G\sum_{j\ne i} m_j\,\frac{r_j - r_i}{|r_j-r_i|^3}\,, \qquad i=1,2,3.$$ Six second-order ODEs in three dimensions: $18$-dimensional phase space. Newton (1687) wrote down the equations; the next two centuries were a search for explicit solutions, and the next century after that was a forced retreat from that search.

The system is Hamiltonian with $H = \sum_i \tfrac{|p_i|^2}{2m_i} - G\sum_{ino further algebraic integrals exist — there is no closed-form solution.

Two-body & three-body simulator
orbit traces; conservation readout
init…

Energy $E$, angular momentum $L_z$, and centre of mass $R$ are conserved exactly by the equations; the readout tracks how well the leapfrog integrator preserves them. The figure-eight initial conditions (Chenciner–Montgomery) are r₁=(0.97,−0.243), r₃=−r₁, r₂=0, with velocities $\dot r_2 = -2\dot r_1 = -2\dot r_3 = (-0.93, 0.864)/2$ and $G=m_i=1$.

See also
  • Orbits, flows, and vector fields

Used in
The three-body problem · Lagrange, Euler, and the figure-eight
The three-body problem · Restricted three-body problem & Lagrange points
</section>

2. Restricted three-body & Lagrange points

Drop the third mass to zero. The two primaries $m_1,m_2$ now decouple — they trace a Keplerian ellipse — and the test particle obeys

$$\ddot r \;=\; -G\,\frac{m_1(r-r_1(t))}{|r-r_1(t)|^3} \;-\; G\,\frac{m_2(r-r_2(t))}{|r-r_2(t)|^3}.$$

In the circular restricted case, the primaries orbit at fixed separation; passing to a frame co-rotating at angular velocity $\Omega$ pins them at constant positions and gives a time-independent effective potential

$$U_{\mathrm{eff}}(x,y) \;=\; -\tfrac{1}{2}\Omega^2(x^2+y^2) \;-\; \frac{Gm_1}{r_1} \;-\; \frac{Gm_2}{r_2}.$$

Critical points $\nabla U_{\mathrm{eff}} = 0$ are equilibria of the full rotating-frame dynamics (the Coriolis force vanishes at zero velocity). They form Lagrange's five points: three collinear $L_1, L_2, L_3$ on the $m_1$–$m_2$ axis (saddles for every mass ratio), and two equilateral $L_4, L_5$ at the apexes of equilateral triangles on the two sides of that axis.

Routh (1875): $L_4$ and $L_5$ are linearly stable (centres) when $\frac{m_1}{m_2} > \frac{25 + 3\sqrt{69}}{2} \approx 24.96$. The Sun–Jupiter ratio is $\approx 1047$, so the equilateral wells trap the Trojans. Earth–Moon is $\approx 81$, also stable; Sun–Earth is $\approx 333{,}000$, comfortably so.

Effective potential & Lagrange points
drag the slider; watch $L_1$–$L_5$ slide
Prereq
  • dynamical-systems — Fixed points and linearization — the same Hartman–Grobman analysis classifies the Lagrange points as saddles vs centres.

See also
  • dynamical-systems — Fixed points and linearization — the same Hartman–Grobman analysis classifies the Lagrange points as saddles vs centres.

Used in
The three-body problem · Poincaré's tangle — the birth of chaos
The three-body problem · Spacecraft trajectories and Trojan asteroids
</section>

3. Lagrange, Euler, and the figure-eight

The full three-body problem, away from the restricted limit, admits only a thin family of explicit solutions. Three classes of relative equilibria — solutions that look stationary in some rotating frame — were known before 1900:

  • Lagrange (1772) — equilateral. Place the three masses at the vertices of an equilateral triangle with side $a$. Choose velocities so the triangle rigidly rotates about the common centre of mass. The configuration stays equilateral forever; in the planar case it can also expand and contract along a Keplerian similarity (homographic motions).
  • Euler (1767) — collinear. Three masses on a rotating line, with separations in fixed ratios determined by a quintic in $m_1,m_2,m_3$. Three orderings of masses give three Euler families.
  • Chenciner–Montgomery (2000) — figure-eight. For three equal masses, there exists a planar orbit on which all three bodies trace a single curve in the shape of a lemniscate, chasing each other with equal time-spacing. Discovered numerically by Moore (1993), proved rigorously by Chenciner and Montgomery via an action minimisation on a symmetry-constrained loop space.
Three special solutions
click a button; watch the orbit

All three are choreographies in a generalised sense: every mass follows a translate (or the same copy) of one curve. The figure-eight is the only currently known choreography of three equal masses on a single curve in the plane that is not a relative equilibrium.

Used in
The three-body problem · Spacecraft trajectories and Trojan asteroids
</section>

4. Poincaré's tangle and the birth of chaos

King Oscar II of Sweden offered a prize in 1885 for an explicit series solution to the $n$-body problem. Poincaré won in 1889 with a memoir on the restricted three-body problem — and then, while preparing the published version, he discovered an error. His correction was deeper than the original work: stable and unstable manifolds of a hyperbolic periodic orbit can intersect transversally, and once they do, they must do so infinitely often.

Poincaré's own description (1899): "When one tries to depict the figure formed by these two curves and their infinitely many intersections... one is struck by the complexity of this figure, which I shall not even attempt to draw." The picture — the homoclinic tangle — is the geometric heart of chaos.

Smale (1965) extracted a topological model: take a unit square, stretch it horizontally by a factor $\lambda > 2$, compress it vertically by $\lambda^{-1}$, fold it into a horseshoe shape, and lay it back inside the original square. The invariant set $\Lambda = \bigcap_{n\in\mathbb{Z}} f^n(Q)$ is a Cantor-like set on which the dynamics is conjugate to the full shift $\sigma$ on $\{0,1\}^{\mathbb{Z}}$:

$$\bigl(\Lambda, f\bigr) \;\cong\; \bigl(\{0,1\}^{\mathbb{Z}},\, \sigma\bigr).$$

Consequences: countably many periodic orbits of every period, a dense orbit, sensitive dependence on initial conditions, positive topological entropy. Wherever a homoclinic tangle exists, a horseshoe is embedded — and so is chaos.

Smale horseshoe — symbolic itinerary
click points; read off the binary code

Each iteration of the horseshoe map cuts the surviving set into thinner horizontal strips. After $n$ iterations forward (and $n$ back) what remains is $2^{2n}$ rectangles indexed by length-$2n$ binary words — the symbolic itinerary $\dots a_{-2}a_{-1}.a_0a_1a_2\dots$ of the Cantor set $\Lambda$.

Prereq
  • dynamical-systems — Poincaré sections and return maps — the horseshoe lives on a Poincaré section transverse to a periodic orbit.
  • dynamical-systems — Chaos and Lyapunov exponents — sensitive dependence + transitivity + dense periodic orbits.

See also
  • dynamical-systems — Poincaré sections and return maps — the horseshoe lives on a Poincaré section transverse to a periodic orbit.
  • dynamical-systems — Chaos and Lyapunov exponents — sensitive dependence + transitivity + dense periodic orbits.

Used in
The three-body problem · KAM theorem and stability of the solar system
</section>

5. KAM and the stability of the solar system

Poincaré's tangle is a worst-case scenario: chaos is generic. But planetary orbits have been quasi-stable for billions of years — so what saves them?

The answer is the Kolmogorov–Arnold–Moser theorem. Start with an integrable Hamiltonian $H_0(I)$ on action-angle variables $(I,\theta)\in\mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{T}^n$ — the phase space is foliated by invariant tori $\{I = \mathrm{const}\}$, on each of which the flow is linear with frequency $\omega(I) = \partial H_0/\partial I$. Perturb:

$$H(I,\theta) \;=\; H_0(I) \;+\; \varepsilon\,H_1(I,\theta).$$

Naively, every torus could break up under arbitrarily small $\varepsilon$. KAM (Kolmogorov 1954, Arnold 1963, Moser 1962) shows otherwise: most tori survive, deformed but topologically intact. The survivors are exactly those whose frequencies $\omega$ are Diophantine: there exist $\gamma > 0,\ \tau > n-1$ with

$$|k\cdot\omega| \;\ge\; \frac{\gamma}{|k|^\tau} \quad\text{for all }0\ne k\in\mathbb{Z}^n.$$

The set of Diophantine $\omega$ has full Lebesgue measure as $\gamma\to 0$, and the proof uses a Newton-style super-convergent iteration to defeat the small-denominator divergences $1/(k\cdot\omega)$ that doomed the classical perturbation series.

Resonant tori (where $k\cdot\omega = 0$ for some $k$) generically dissolve into Birkhoff zones of alternating elliptic and hyperbolic islands. Between the surviving KAM tori, chaos can leak through (Arnold diffusion); but for $n=2$ degrees of freedom, KAM tori topologically enclose the chaotic regions and bound them forever.

KAM: tori vs. perturbation
drag $\varepsilon$; watch resonances destroy tori first

Schematic: each ring is an invariant torus indexed by its rotation number $\rho = \omega_2/\omega_1$. Rational $\rho$ resonates first — the torus breaks into a Birkhoff chain of islands. Irrational $\rho$ with high Diophantine constant survive longest. The golden mean $\rho = (\sqrt{5}-1)/2$ is the most robust (Greene's residue criterion).

Prereq
  • symplectic-manifolds — Hamiltonian vector fields and flows — KAM is a theorem about Hamiltonian perturbations of integrable systems on a symplectic manifold.

See also
  • symplectic-manifolds — Hamiltonian vector fields and flows — KAM is a theorem about Hamiltonian perturbations of integrable systems on a symplectic manifold.
  • Lebesgue measure
</section>

6. Spacecraft, Trojans, and exoplanet stability

Three-body dynamics is not academic. The Lagrange points are real estate; the homoclinic web is a transit network; KAM bounds tell us which exoplanet architectures can persist.

Mission / objectWhereThree-body role
JWSTSun–Earth $L_2$ halo$L_2$ saddle exploited; passive shading from sun, earth, moon
SOHO, ACE, DSCOVRSun–Earth $L_1$continuous solar-wind monitor between sun and earth
LRO, lunar gatewayEarth–Moon $L_1, L_2$cislunar staging on near-rectilinear halo orbits
Voyager 1 / 2Jupiter, Saturn flybysgravity assists $\Rightarrow$ solar-system escape
Trojan asteroidsSun–Jupiter $L_4, L_5$$\sim 10^4$ bodies librating in equilateral wells
Genesis (2001)Sun–Earth $L_1$ & $L_2$low-energy transfer along stable manifolds (Koon–Lo–Marsden–Ross)

The interplanetary transport network, formalised by Koon, Lo, Marsden, and Ross around 2000, exploits the same homoclinic tangle Poincaré described to design near-zero-fuel transfers between planets. Trajectories ride invariant manifolds of $L_1$/$L_2$ Lyapunov orbits; intersections of stable and unstable manifolds in different three-body subsystems splice into a multi-planet itinerary.

For exoplanets, KAM and resonance overlap criteria (Wisdom, Murray–Holman) bound which multi-planet architectures are dynamically stable on Gyr timescales. Mean-motion resonances (e.g. Trappist-1's $8\!:\!5\!:\!3\!:\!2\!:\!3\!:\!4\!:\!3$ Laplace chain) sit on the boundary between order and chaos — protected by KAM tori on one side, eaten by Arnold diffusion on the other.

Halo orbit at $L_1$ / $L_2$
click a Lagrange point; integrate locally

Halo orbits at $L_1$/$L_2$ are 3D periodic orbits in the rotating frame; tadpole orbits at $L_4$ are large-amplitude librations. Real spacecraft halo orbits (e.g. JWST's around Sun–Earth $L_2$) have amplitudes $\sim 10^5$ km — small fractions of the Earth–Sun distance.
</section>

Connections

The three-body problem is unsolved in a precise sense: no algebraic first integrals beyond the classical ten exist (Bruns, Poincaré), and Sundman's 1912 convergent series — though formally giving a global solution — converges so slowly that no useful number ever emerges. The questions that remain open are surprising: are all bounded planar solutions either Lagrangian, Euler, or chaotic? do new choreographies with more bodies exist generically? how thick is Arnold diffusion in the real solar system, and can it explain Mercury's marginal long-term stability?

  • dynamical-systems — Chaos and Lyapunov exponents — the qualitative theory Poincaré invented to handle this problem.
  • symplectic-manifolds — Hamiltonian flows — the geometric stage on which KAM is proved.
  • symplectic-manifolds — Poisson bracket — energy and angular momentum Poisson-commute, encoding the conservation laws.
  • hamiltonians-classical-mechanics — action-angle variables and integrable systems are the backdrop KAM perturbs.
  • morse-theory — Critical points and Morse functions — the variational construction of the figure-eight is Morse theory on a constrained loop space.

/* ========================== Widget 1: N-body simulator ========================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tbp-sim");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tbp-sim-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const colors = ["var(--yellow)","var(--cyan)","var(--pink)"];
  let bodies = [];
  let trails = [];
  let running = true;
  let speed = 6;
  const G = 1.0;

  const PRESETS = {
    "2body": () => {
      const v = Math.sqrt(G*1.0/1.5);
      return [
        {m:1.0, x:-0.5, y:0, vx:0, vy:-v*0.5},
        {m:1.0, x: 1.0, y:0, vx:0, vy: v*0.5},
        {m:0.0, x:0,    y:0, vx:0, vy:0}
      ];
    },
    "equilateral": () => {
      const a = 1.5;
      const r = a/Math.sqrt(3);
      const omega = Math.sqrt(G*3/(a*a*a));
      const pts = [
        {x:r*Math.cos(Math.PI/2), y:r*Math.sin(Math.PI/2)},
        {x:r*Math.cos(Math.PI/2 + 2*Math.PI/3), y:r*Math.sin(Math.PI/2 + 2*Math.PI/3)},
        {x:r*Math.cos(Math.PI/2 + 4*Math.PI/3), y:r*Math.sin(Math.PI/2 + 4*Math.PI/3)}
      ];
      return pts.map(p=>({m:1.0, x:p.x, y:p.y, vx:-omega*p.y, vy:omega*p.x}));
    },
    "eight": () => {
      const x1=0.97000436, y1=-0.24308753;
      const vx=0.93240737/2, vy=0.86473146/2;
      return [
        {m:1.0, x: x1, y: y1, vx: vx, vy: vy},
        {m:1.0, x:-x1, y:-y1, vx: vx, vy: vy},
        {m:1.0, x: 0,  y: 0,  vx:-2*vx, vy:-2*vy}
      ];
    },
    "chaotic": () => [
      {m:1.0, x:-1.0, y: 0.2, vx: 0.1,  vy: 0.5},
      {m:1.0, x: 1.0, y: 0.0, vx: 0.0,  vy:-0.5},
      {m:1.0, x: 0.0, y:-0.6, vx:-0.2,  vy: 0.1}
    ]
  };

  let currentPreset = "2body";
  function reset(){
    bodies = PRESETS[currentPreset]().map(b => Object.assign({}, b));
    trails = bodies.map(()=>[]);
    initialEnergy = totalEnergy();
    initialL = totalL();
  }

  function totalEnergy(){
    let K=0, U=0;
    for(let i=0;i<bodies.length;i++){
      K += 0.5*bodies[i].m*(bodies[i].vx*bodies[i].vx + bodies[i].vy*bodies[i].vy);
      for(let j=i+1;j<bodies.length;j++){
        const dx = bodies[i].x-bodies[j].x, dy = bodies[i].y-bodies[j].y;
        const r = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
        if(r>1e-6 && bodies[i].m>0 && bodies[j].m>0) U -= G*bodies[i].m*bodies[j].m/r;
      }
    }
    return K+U;
  }
  function totalL(){
    let L=0;
    for(const b of bodies) L += b.m*(b.x*b.vy - b.y*b.vx);
    return L;
  }

  function accel(i){
    let ax=0, ay=0;
    for(let j=0;j<bodies.length;j++){
      if(j===i) continue;
      const dx = bodies[j].x-bodies[i].x, dy = bodies[j].y-bodies[i].y;
      const r2 = dx*dx+dy*dy;
      if(r2 < 0.0025) continue;
      const r3 = r2*Math.sqrt(r2);
      ax += G*bodies[j].m*dx/r3;
      ay += G*bodies[j].m*dy/r3;
    }
    return [ax,ay];
  }

  function step(dt){
    const ax = bodies.map((_,i)=>accel(i));
    for(let i=0;i<bodies.length;i++){
      bodies[i].x += bodies[i].vx*dt + 0.5*ax[i][0]*dt*dt;
      bodies[i].y += bodies[i].vy*dt + 0.5*ax[i][1]*dt*dt;
    }
    const ax2 = bodies.map((_,i)=>accel(i));
    for(let i=0;i<bodies.length;i++){
      bodies[i].vx += 0.5*(ax[i][0]+ax2[i][0])*dt;
      bodies[i].vy += 0.5*(ax[i][1]+ax2[i][1])*dt;
    }
  }

  let initialEnergy=0, initialL=0;
  reset();

  const SCALE = 80;
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    for(let i=0;i<bodies.length;i++){
      if(bodies[i].m<=0) continue;
      if(trails[i].length>2){
        let d = `M${trails[i][0][0]} ${trails[i][0][1]}`;
        for(let k=1;k<trails[i].length;k++) d += ` L${trails[i][k][0]} ${trails[i][k][1]}`;
        const p = SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:colors[i], "stroke-width":1, "stroke-opacity":0.6});
        svg.appendChild(p);
      }
    }
    for(let i=0;i<bodies.length;i++){
      if(bodies[i].m<=0) continue;
      const r = 4 + 2*Math.log(1+bodies[i].m);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:bodies[i].x*SCALE, cy:-bodies[i].y*SCALE, r, fill:colors[i], stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":0.8}));
    }
    const E = totalEnergy(), L = totalL();
    const dE = initialEnergy === 0 ? 0 : (E-initialEnergy)/Math.abs(initialEnergy);
    const dL = initialL === 0 ? 0 : (L-initialL)/Math.abs(initialL);
    readout.textContent = `preset=${currentPreset}  E=${E.toFixed(4)} (ΔE/E=${dE.toExponential(1)})  L_z=${L.toFixed(4)} (ΔL/L=${dL.toExponential(1)})`;
  }

  let last = performance.now();
  function loop(now){
    const dtReal = Math.min(0.04, (now-last)/1000);
    last = now;
    if(running){
      const dt = 0.002;
      const steps = Math.floor(speed*dtReal/dt);
      for(let s=0;s<steps;s++){
        step(dt);
        for(let i=0;i<bodies.length;i++){
          if(bodies[i].m>0) trails[i].push([bodies[i].x*SCALE, -bodies[i].y*SCALE]);
          if(trails[i].length>1200) trails[i].shift();
        }
      }
    }
    draw();
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }
  requestAnimationFrame(loop);

  document.getElementById("tbp-sim-toggle").addEventListener("click", e=>{
    running = !running;
    e.target.textContent = running ? "pause" : "resume";
  });
  document.getElementById("tbp-sim-reset").addEventListener("click", reset);
  document.getElementById("tbp-sim-speed").addEventListener("input", e=>{ speed = +e.target.value; });
  ["2body","equilateral","eight","chaotic"].forEach(k=>{
    document.getElementById("tbp-sim-preset-"+k).addEventListener("click", e=>{
      currentPreset = k;
      [...document.querySelectorAll("[id^=tbp-sim-preset-]")].forEach(b=>b.classList.remove("active"));
      e.target.classList.add("active");
      reset();
    });
  });
})();

/* ========================== Widget 2: Lagrange points ========================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tbp-l");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tbp-l-readout");
  const slider = document.getElementById("tbp-l-mu");
  const muRO = document.getElementById("tbp-l-mu-readout");
  const cbContours = document.getElementById("tbp-l-contours");
  const cbZVC = document.getElementById("tbp-l-zvc");
  if(!svg) return;
  const SCALE = 110;

  function lagrange(mu){
    function bisect(f, a, b){
      let fa=f(a), fb=f(b);
      for(let k=0;k<60 && Math.abs(b-a)>1e-7;k++){
        const m=(a+b)/2, fm=f(m);
        if(fa*fm<=0){ b=m; fb=fm; } else { a=m; fa=fm; }
      }
      return (a+b)/2;
    }
    const dU = x => x - (1-mu)*(x+mu)/Math.pow(Math.abs(x+mu),3) - mu*(x-1+mu)/Math.pow(Math.abs(x-1+mu),3);
    const L1 = bisect(dU, -mu+1e-3, 1-mu-1e-3);
    const L2 = bisect(dU, 1-mu+1e-3, 2);
    const L3 = bisect(dU, -2, -mu-1e-3);
    const L4 = {x: 0.5 - mu, y: Math.sqrt(3)/2};
    const L5 = {x: 0.5 - mu, y: -Math.sqrt(3)/2};
    return {
      L1:{x:L1,y:0}, L2:{x:L2,y:0}, L3:{x:L3,y:0}, L4, L5,
      m1:{x:-mu,y:0}, m2:{x:1-mu,y:0}
    };
  }

  function Ueff(x,y,mu){
    const r1 = Math.hypot(x+mu, y);
    const r2 = Math.hypot(x-1+mu, y);
    return -0.5*(x*x+y*y) - (1-mu)/Math.max(r1,1e-3) - mu/Math.max(r2,1e-3);
  }

  function draw(){
    const mu = +slider.value;
    muRO.textContent = `μ=${mu.toFixed(3)}; mass ratio m₁/m₂=${((1-mu)/mu).toFixed(2)}; Routh threshold = 24.96 ⇒ L₄,₅ ${(1-mu)/mu>24.96?"stable":"unstable"}`;
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);

    if(cbContours.checked){
      const NX=120, NY=80;
      const xmin=-1.6, xmax=1.6, ymin=-1.1, ymax=1.1;
      const grid = [];
      for(let j=0;j<NY;j++){
        const row=[];
        for(let i=0;i<NX;i++){
          const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/(NX-1);
          const y = ymin + j*(ymax-ymin)/(NY-1);
          row.push(Math.max(-3.5, Math.min(-1.5, Ueff(x,y,mu))));
        }
        grid.push(row);
      }
      const levels = [-3.2,-3.0,-2.8,-2.6,-2.4,-2.2,-2.0,-1.85,-1.7,-1.6];
      for(const L of levels){
        let pathD = "";
        for(let j=0;j<NY-1;j++){
          for(let i=0;i<NX-1;i++){
            const v00=grid[j][i], v10=grid[j][i+1], v01=grid[j+1][i], v11=grid[j+1][i+1];
            const x0=xmin+i*(xmax-xmin)/(NX-1), x1=xmin+(i+1)*(xmax-xmin)/(NX-1);
            const y0=ymin+j*(ymax-ymin)/(NY-1), y1=ymin+(j+1)*(ymax-ymin)/(NY-1);
            const above = [v00>L, v10>L, v11>L, v01>L];
            const idx = (above[0]?1:0)|(above[1]?2:0)|(above[2]?4:0)|(above[3]?8:0);
            if(idx===0||idx===15) continue;
            const lerp = (a,b,va,vb)=>{ const t=(L-va)/(vb-va); return [a+(b-a)*t]; };
            const ts = (va,vb)=>(L-va)/(vb-va);
            const e = [
              [x0+ts(v00,v10)*(x1-x0), y0],
              [x1, y0+ts(v10,v11)*(y1-y0)],
              [x0+ts(v01,v11)*(x1-x0), y1],
              [x0, y0+ts(v00,v01)*(y1-y0)]
            ];
            const cases = {1:[3,0],2:[0,1],3:[3,1],4:[1,2],5:[3,2,0,1],6:[0,2],7:[3,2],
                           8:[2,3],9:[2,0],10:[2,3,0,1],11:[2,1],12:[1,3],13:[1,0],14:[0,3]};
            const segs = cases[idx]||[];
            for(let s=0;s<segs.length;s+=2){
              const A = e[segs[s]], B = e[segs[s+1]];
              pathD += `M${A[0]*SCALE} ${-A[1]*SCALE} L${B[0]*SCALE} ${-B[1]*SCALE} `;
            }
          }
        }
        if(pathD) svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:pathD, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":0.5,"stroke-opacity":0.5}));
      }
    }

    const pts = lagrange(mu);
    if(cbZVC.checked){
      const Cstar = -1.55;
      const NX=120, NY=80;
      const xmin=-1.6, xmax=1.6, ymin=-1.1, ymax=1.1;
      let pathD = "";
      for(let j=0;j<NY;j++){
        for(let i=0;i<NX;i++){
          const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/(NX-1);
          const y = ymin + j*(ymax-ymin)/(NY-1);
          if(Ueff(x,y,mu) > Cstar){
            pathD += `M${x*SCALE} ${-y*SCALE} l1 0 `;
          }
        }
      }
      if(pathD) svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:pathD, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":1.2,"stroke-opacity":0.18}));
    }

    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:pts.m1.x*SCALE, cy:-pts.m1.y*SCALE, r:8, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:pts.m2.x*SCALE, cy:-pts.m2.y*SCALE, r:5, fill:"var(--cyan)"}));
    const t1 = SVG("text",{x:pts.m1.x*SCALE-12, y:-pts.m1.y*SCALE-12, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"}); t1.textContent = "m₁"; svg.appendChild(t1);
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:pts.m2.x*SCALE+8, y:-pts.m2.y*SCALE-8, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"}); t2.textContent = "m₂"; svg.appendChild(t2);

    [["L1",pts.L1,"var(--pink)"],["L2",pts.L2,"var(--pink)"],["L3",pts.L3,"var(--pink)"],
     ["L4",pts.L4,"var(--green)"],["L5",pts.L5,"var(--green)"]].forEach(([name,p,col])=>{
       svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:p.x*SCALE, cy:-p.y*SCALE, r:5, fill:col, stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":1}));
       const tx = SVG("text",{x:p.x*SCALE+8, y:-p.y*SCALE+4,"font-size":11,fill:col,"font-weight":600});
       tx.textContent = name; svg.appendChild(tx);
    });

    readout.textContent = `L₁=(${pts.L1.x.toFixed(3)},0)  L₂=(${pts.L2.x.toFixed(3)},0)  L₃=(${pts.L3.x.toFixed(3)},0)  L₄=(${pts.L4.x.toFixed(3)},${pts.L4.y.toFixed(3)})  L₅=(${pts.L5.x.toFixed(3)},${pts.L5.y.toFixed(3)})`;
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", draw);
  cbContours.addEventListener("change", draw);
  cbZVC.addEventListener("change", draw);
  draw();
})();

/* ========================== Widget 3: Special solutions ========================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tbp-sp");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tbp-sp-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const SCALE = 80;
  const colors = ["var(--yellow)","var(--cyan)","var(--pink)"];
  let mode = "eq";
  let t = 0;
  let trails = [[],[],[]];

  function positions(t){
    if(mode === "eq"){
      const a = 1.5;
      const r = a/Math.sqrt(3);
      const om = Math.sqrt(3)/(a*Math.sqrt(a));
      return [0,1,2].map(i=>{
        const ang = Math.PI/2 + i*2*Math.PI/3 + om*t;
        return [r*Math.cos(ang), r*Math.sin(ang)];
      });
    }
    if(mode === "col"){
      const om = 1.0;
      const ang = om*t;
      const c = Math.cos(ang), s = Math.sin(ang);
      return [[-1.5*c,-1.5*s],[0,0],[1.5*c,1.5*s]];
    }
    if(mode === "eight"){
      const T = 6.3259;
      const phase = (t % T)/T;
      const u = phase * 2*Math.PI;
      const a = 1.0, b = 0.45;
      const denom = 1 + Math.sin(u)*Math.sin(u);
      const x = a*Math.cos(u)/denom;
      const y = b*Math.sin(2*u)/denom;
      return [
        [x, y],
        [a*Math.cos(u + 2*Math.PI/3)/(1 + Math.sin(u+2*Math.PI/3)**2), b*Math.sin(2*(u+2*Math.PI/3))/(1+Math.sin(u+2*Math.PI/3)**2)],
        [a*Math.cos(u + 4*Math.PI/3)/(1 + Math.sin(u+4*Math.PI/3)**2), b*Math.sin(2*(u+4*Math.PI/3))/(1+Math.sin(u+4*Math.PI/3)**2)]
      ];
    }
    return [[0,0],[0,0],[0,0]];
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const xax = SVG("line",{x1:-200,y1:0,x2:200,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.5});
    svg.appendChild(xax);
    for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
      if(trails[i].length>2){
        let d = `M${trails[i][0][0]} ${trails[i][0][1]}`;
        for(let k=1;k<trails[i].length;k++) d += ` L${trails[i][k][0]} ${trails[i][k][1]}`;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:colors[i], "stroke-width":1.5, "stroke-opacity":0.5}));
      }
    }
    const pos = positions(t);
    for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:pos[i][0]*SCALE, cy:-pos[i][1]*SCALE, r:6, fill:colors[i], stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":0.8}));
    }
    const labels = {eq:"Lagrange equilateral (rotating triangle)", col:"Euler collinear (3 masses on a rotating line)", eight:"figure-eight (Chenciner–Montgomery 2000)"};
    readout.textContent = `${labels[mode]}  t=${t.toFixed(2)}`;
  }

  function reset(){ trails = [[],[],[]]; t=0; }

  let last = performance.now();
  function loop(now){
    const dt = Math.min(0.05, (now-last)/1000);
    last = now;
    t += dt*0.6;
    const pos = positions(t);
    for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
      trails[i].push([pos[i][0]*SCALE, -pos[i][1]*SCALE]);
      if(trails[i].length>800) trails[i].shift();
    }
    draw();
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }
  requestAnimationFrame(loop);

  document.getElementById("tbp-sp-eq").addEventListener("click", e=>{ mode="eq"; reset(); setActive(e.target); });
  document.getElementById("tbp-sp-col").addEventListener("click", e=>{ mode="col"; reset(); setActive(e.target); });
  document.getElementById("tbp-sp-eight").addEventListener("click", e=>{ mode="eight"; reset(); setActive(e.target); });
  function setActive(btn){
    ["tbp-sp-eq","tbp-sp-col","tbp-sp-eight"].forEach(id=>document.getElementById(id).classList.remove("active"));
    btn.classList.add("active");
  }
})();

/* ========================== Widget 4: Smale horseshoe ========================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tbp-h");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tbp-h-readout");
  const iter = document.getElementById("tbp-h-iter");
  if(!svg) return;

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const n = +iter.value;
    const S = 100;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:-S, y:-S, width:2*S, height:2*S, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.2,"stroke-dasharray":"4 3"}));
    const labelQ = SVG("text",{x:-S, y:-S-6,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"}); labelQ.textContent = "Q (unit square)"; svg.appendChild(labelQ);

    const stripsH = Math.pow(2, n);
    const w = (2*S)/stripsH;
    for(let k=0;k<stripsH;k++){
      const code = k.toString(2).padStart(n,"0");
      const isLeft = code[0]==="0";
      const fill = isLeft ? "var(--cyan)" : "var(--pink)";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:-S+k*w, y:-S, width:w, height:2*S, fill, "fill-opacity":0.18, stroke:fill,"stroke-width":0.5}));
    }

    const stripsV = Math.pow(2, n);
    const h = (2*S)/stripsV;
    for(let k=0;k<stripsV;k++){
      const code = k.toString(2).padStart(n,"0");
      const isTop = code[0]==="0";
      const fill = isTop ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--green)";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:-S, y:-S+k*h, width:2*S, height:h, fill, "fill-opacity":0.10, stroke:fill,"stroke-width":0.4}));
    }

    const total = Math.pow(4, n);
    let bin = "";
    for(let i=0;i<n;i++) bin += "0";
    bin += ".";
    for(let i=0;i<n;i++) bin += "1";
    readout.textContent = `iterations n=${n}  |Λ_n|=${total} surviving rectangles  example itinerary: …${bin}…  (Λ ≅ {0,1}^Z under conjugation by σ)`;
  }
  iter.addEventListener("input", draw);
  document.getElementById("tbp-h-step").addEventListener("click", ()=>{ iter.value = Math.min(6, +iter.value + 1); draw(); });
  document.getElementById("tbp-h-back").addEventListener("click", ()=>{ iter.value = Math.max(0, +iter.value - 1); draw(); });
  document.getElementById("tbp-h-reset").addEventListener("click", ()=>{ iter.value = 0; draw(); });
  draw();
})();

/* ========================== Widget 5: KAM tori ========================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tbp-k");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tbp-k-readout");
  const epsSlider = document.getElementById("tbp-k-eps");
  const epsRO = document.getElementById("tbp-k-eps-readout");
  if(!svg) return;

  function diophantineConst(rho){
    let best = Infinity;
    for(let q=1;q<=40;q++){
      const p = Math.round(rho*q);
      if(q===0) continue;
      const d = Math.abs(rho - p/q);
      if(d > 0) best = Math.min(best, d * q*q);
    }
    return best;
  }

  function draw(){
    const eps = +epsSlider.value;
    epsRO.textContent = `ε=${eps.toFixed(2)}`;
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);

    const tori = [];
    for(let n=1;n<=20;n++){
      const rho = (n / 21) * 1.0;
      tori.push({rho, R: 30 + n*5});
    }
    tori.push({rho:(Math.sqrt(5)-1)/2, R: 30 + 21*5, golden:true});

    for(const tor of tori){
      const dc = diophantineConst(tor.rho);
      const breakEps = Math.max(0.01, dc*2.5);
      const broken = eps > breakEps;
      if(!broken){
        const pts = [];
        for(let k=0;k<=64;k++){
          const a = k/64*2*Math.PI;
          const wob = eps*0.4*Math.sin(3*a + tor.rho*8);
          pts.push([(tor.R+wob*tor.R*0.05)*Math.cos(a), (tor.R+wob*tor.R*0.05)*Math.sin(a)]);
        }
        let d = `M${pts[0][0]} ${pts[0][1]}`;
        for(let k=1;k<pts.length;k++) d += ` L${pts[k][0]} ${pts[k][1]}`;
        d += " Z";
        const col = tor.golden ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--cyan)";
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:col, "stroke-width": tor.golden?1.6:0.9, "stroke-opacity":0.85}));
      } else {
        const islands = Math.max(3, Math.round(1/Math.max(dc,0.02)));
        const cap = Math.min(islands, 13);
        for(let k=0;k<cap;k++){
          const a = k/cap*2*Math.PI;
          const ix = tor.R*Math.cos(a), iy = tor.R*Math.sin(a);
          svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx:ix, cy:iy, rx:6, ry:3, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":0.8,"stroke-opacity":0.55,
            transform:`rotate(${(a*180/Math.PI)+90} ${ix} ${iy})`}));
        }
      }
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:0, cy:0, r:3, fill:"var(--ink)"}));
    const txt = SVG("text",{x:0,y:-130,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"middle"});
    txt.textContent = "rings = invariant tori; pink islands = broken (resonant) tori; gold = golden-mean torus";
    svg.appendChild(txt);
    const surviving = tori.filter(t => eps <= Math.max(0.01, diophantineConst(t.rho)*2.5)).length;
    readout.textContent = `ε=${eps.toFixed(2)}  surviving tori: ${surviving}/${tori.length}  (resonant tori break first; the golden-mean torus is most robust — Greene's residue criterion)`;
  }
  epsSlider.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();

/* ========================== Widget 6: Halo orbits ========================== */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tbp-h2");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tbp-h2-readout");
  const ampSlider = document.getElementById("tbp-h2-amp");
  if(!svg) return;
  const SCALE = 200;
  const mu = 3.0e-6;
  let mode = "L1";
  let t = 0;

  function lagrangeColl(mu){
    const dU = x => x - (1-mu)*(x+mu)/Math.pow(Math.abs(x+mu),3) - mu*(x-1+mu)/Math.pow(Math.abs(x-1+mu),3);
    function bisect(a,b){ let fa=dU(a); for(let k=0;k<60;k++){ const m=(a+b)/2; const fm=dU(m); if(fa*fm<=0){b=m;} else {a=m;fa=fm;} } return (a+b)/2; }
    return { L1: bisect(-mu+1e-3, 1-mu-1e-3), L2: bisect(1-mu+1e-3, 2) };
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const amp = +ampSlider.value;
    const Ls = lagrangeColl(mu);
    const x_origin_view = mode === "L4" ? 0 : (1 - mu);
    const earthScreenX = (1-mu - x_origin_view) * SCALE * 50;
    const sunScreenX = (-mu - x_origin_view) * SCALE * 50;
    const view_scale = mode === "L4" ? 0.4 : 50;

    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx: (-mu - x_origin_view)*SCALE*view_scale, cy:0, r:8, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx: (1-mu - x_origin_view)*SCALE*view_scale, cy:0, r:5, fill:"var(--cyan)"}));
    const tSun = SVG("text",{x:(-mu - x_origin_view)*SCALE*view_scale - 14, y:-12,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tSun.textContent = "Sun"; svg.appendChild(tSun);
    const tEarth = SVG("text",{x:(1-mu - x_origin_view)*SCALE*view_scale + 8, y:-10,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tEarth.textContent = "Earth"; svg.appendChild(tEarth);

    let centerX, label;
    if(mode === "L1"){ centerX = (Ls.L1 - x_origin_view) * SCALE * view_scale; label = "L₁"; }
    else if(mode === "L2"){ centerX = (Ls.L2 - x_origin_view) * SCALE * view_scale; label = "L₂"; }
    else { centerX = (0.5-mu)*SCALE*view_scale; label = "L₄"; }
    const centerY = mode === "L4" ? -Math.sqrt(3)/2*SCALE*view_scale : 0;

    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:centerX, cy:centerY, r:3, fill:"var(--pink)"}));
    const lTx = SVG("text",{x:centerX+6, y:centerY-6,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--pink)","font-weight":600});
    lTx.textContent = label; svg.appendChild(lTx);

    const NPTS = 200;
    let d = "";
    if(mode === "L4"){
      for(let k=0;k<=NPTS;k++){
        const phase = (k/NPTS)*2*Math.PI;
        const wobble = amp * 4;
        const x = centerX + wobble*SCALE*view_scale*Math.cos(phase) * 1.5;
        const y = centerY + wobble*SCALE*view_scale*Math.sin(phase) * 0.6;
        d += (k===0?"M":"L") + x.toFixed(2) + " " + y.toFixed(2) + " ";
      }
    } else {
      for(let k=0;k<=NPTS;k++){
        const phase = (k/NPTS)*2*Math.PI;
        const A_x = amp * SCALE * view_scale * 0.6;
        const A_y = amp * SCALE * view_scale * 1.4;
        const x = centerX + A_x*Math.cos(phase);
        const y = centerY + A_y*Math.sin(phase);
        d += (k===0?"M":"L") + x.toFixed(2) + " " + y.toFixed(2) + " ";
      }
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--green)","stroke-width":1.4}));

    const phase = t;
    let sx, sy;
    if(mode === "L4"){
      sx = centerX + amp*SCALE*view_scale*1.5*Math.cos(phase);
      sy = centerY + amp*SCALE*view_scale*0.6*Math.sin(phase);
    } else {
      sx = centerX + amp*SCALE*view_scale*0.6*Math.cos(phase);
      sy = centerY + amp*SCALE*view_scale*1.4*Math.sin(phase);
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx, cy:sy, r:3.5, fill:"var(--violet)", stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":0.8}));

    const realKm = mode === "L4" ? "tadpole libration ~150°" : `amplitude ≈ ${(amp*1.496e8).toFixed(0)} km`;
    readout.textContent = `${label} ${mode==="L4"?"tadpole libration":"halo orbit"}  ${realKm}  (μ = m_Earth/(m_Sun+m_Earth) = 3.0×10⁻⁶)`;
  }

  let last = performance.now();
  function loop(now){
    const dt = Math.min(0.05, (now-last)/1000);
    last = now;
    t += dt*0.7;
    draw();
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }
  requestAnimationFrame(loop);

  document.getElementById("tbp-h2-l1").addEventListener("click", e=>{ mode="L1"; setActive(e.target); });
  document.getElementById("tbp-h2-l2").addEventListener("click", e=>{ mode="L2"; setActive(e.target); });
  document.getElementById("tbp-h2-l4").addEventListener("click", e=>{ mode="L4"; setActive(e.target); });
  ampSlider.addEventListener("input", draw);
  function setActive(btn){
    ["tbp-h2-l1","tbp-h2-l2","tbp-h2-l4"].forEach(id=>document.getElementById(id).classList.remove("active"));
    btn.classList.add("active");
  }
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>
